- Backlog Item ID: BI-260017
- Title: Formalize delivery and quality baseline
- Persona/Actor: Product owner, frontend team, and domain operators
- Value statement: For predictable MVP release quality, define explicit acceptance, reliability, observability, and fallback standards before shipping.
- Context: Delivery-and-quality decision phase for I1-I6.
- Problem statement: Without explicit quality gates and fallback rules, release decisions can become subjective and trust-damaging regressions may pass.
- Expected outcome: Team can release MVP with measurable quality standards and reversible operations.
- Success metric(s):
  - I1-I6 are accepted and encoded in formal artifacts.
  - FR/QR/OR/CR requirements define delivery and quality gates.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - ADR-260020 is created and accepted.
  - REQ-FR-260019, REQ-QR-260002, REQ-OR-260005, and REQ-CR-260009 are created and linked.
  - Product backlog and backlog status include BI-260017 with traceability.
- Non-goals: full-scale enterprise observability platform rollout and complete non-core page implementation in MVP.
- Constraints: Preserve accepted product scope and architecture boundaries while enforcing release quality gates.
- Dependencies: Accepted A-H decisions and baseline CI/release workflow.
- Risks/assumptions:
  - Risk: insufficient instrumentation coverage may hide high-impact defects.
  - Risk: missing rollback rehearsal may delay incident recovery.
  - Assumption: six critical flows provide enough release confidence for MVP.
- Validation approach: quality-gate checks, observability checks, accessibility/reliability checks, and rollback rehearsal checks.
- Priority score: 89
- Target window: MVP foundation
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260019.md, REQ-QR-260002.md, REQ-OR-260005.md, REQ-CR-260009.md
  - ADR: ADR-260020.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - Changeset: n/a
